2024-04-24.abstract: For residents of Ukraine’s second-largest city, daily Russian attacks have escalated fears but have not brought life to a standstill.

2024-04-24.title: Aid to Ukraine Is on the Way. Here’s How It Might Help.
2024-04-24.abstract: Weapons from the support package, considered “a lifeline” for Ukraine’s military, could be arriving on the battlefield within days.

2024-04-25.title: What to Know About Venice’s Fees for Day Trips
2024-04-25.abstract: Venice is trying to mitigate overtourism with a small fee on busy days. City leaders hope it will make visitors more aware of the city’s fragility.

2024-04-25.title: Blinken Tours China to Promote Some Ties, While the U.S. Severs Others
2024-04-25.abstract: Tensions over economic ties are running high, threatening to disrupt a fragile cooperation between the U.S. and China.

2024-04-25.title: These Issues Are Testing the U.S.-China Thaw
2024-04-25.abstract: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken is in China this week as tensions have risen over trade, security, Russia’s war on Ukraine and the Middle East crisis.

2024-04-25.title: ‘We’re Aware of the Location’: Aid Groups in Gaza Coordinated With I.D.F. but Still Came Under Fire
2024-04-25.abstract: Visual evidence and internal communications obtained by The Times show six aid groups based in Western countries, including Israel’s strongest allies, had humanitarian sites hit by Israeli strikes, even after the locations were shared with the I.D.F.

2024-04-25.title: Why This Small Ukrainian Hilltop Town Is Russia’s Next Big Target
2024-04-25.abstract: Chasiv Yar has been under relentless attack by Russian forces. Controlling the town would put them in striking distance of key Ukrainian operational and supply centers.

2024-04-26.title: The Harley Riders With a Taste for Tea and Apple Strudel
2024-04-26.abstract: The Harley Owners Group in Alice Springs is what’s known as a social motorcycle club, with the emphasis on social.

2024-04-26.title: Save the Children Says Its Guatemala Offices Were Searched Over Abuse Claims
2024-04-26.abstract: Prosecutors in Guatemala, which has a history of targeting nonprofit groups, said they were probing claims of child abuse, which the charity denied.

2024-04-26.title: Xi and Blinken Trade Small Nods Over a Large Gap
2024-04-26.abstract: The U.S. secretary of state and the Chinese leader struck conciliatory notes in Beijing. But there was no budging on, or hiding, their governments’ core differences.

2024-04-26.title: When U.S. Diplomats Visit China, Meal Choices Are About More Than Taste Buds
2024-04-26.abstract: Visits to China by American officials like Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken can bring fame to local restaurants, as well as scrutiny to the dignitaries.

2024-04-26.title: Has South Africa Truly Defeated Apartheid?
2024-04-26.abstract: We looked at how far South Africa has come in meeting its goals after 30 years of freedom.

2024-04-27.title: Suddenly, Chinese Spies Seem to Be Popping Up All Over Europe
2024-04-27.abstract: A flurry of arrests this week reflect the continent’s newly toughened response to Beijing’s espionage activities and political meddling.

2024-04-27.title: Putin’s War Will Soon Reach Russians’ Tax Bills
2024-04-27.abstract: Russia’s president has signaled an increase in income and corporate taxes that will help finance the war. The move reflects his firm control over Russian policy.

2024-04-27.title: From a Heavy Metal Band in Hijabs, a Message of Girl Power
2024-04-27.abstract: Voice of Baceprot has electrified audiences and built a large following in Indonesia. Now the group is taking its music to the West.

2024-04-27.title: A City Scarred by Terrorism Prepares an Olympic Opening Without Walls
2024-04-27.abstract: The opening ceremony for this summer’s Paris Games will be held outside a stadium — an Olympics first. Making it safe is complicated.

2024-04-27.title: How the U.S. Humanitarian Pier in Gaza Will Work
2024-04-27.abstract: A pier operation being assembled by U.S. service members will involve an elaborate process to provide Gazans with just a portion of the aid they need.

2024-04-28.title: Tornado Kills 5 in Guangzhou, a Chinese City Battered by Recent Rains
2024-04-28.abstract: Videos shared by Chinese official media showed transmission towers and power lines igniting and debris swirling in the air in the city, a manufacturing and technology hub by the Pearl River.

2024-04-28.title: Asylum Seekers Already in U.K. Say Rwanda Law Creates New Anxiety
2024-04-28.abstract: For the tens of thousands of asylum seekers in Britain, a new law brings the possibility of deportation to central Africa closer. We asked how it was affecting them.

2024-04-28.title: Pope’s Visit to Art Exhibition in Prison Is a First for Venice Biennale
2024-04-28.abstract: Incarcerated women serve as guides to the show, which reflects Pope Francis’ longtime commitment to society’s marginalized people.

2024-04-28.title: How Fentanyl Laid Waste to Guatemala’s Time-Worn Opium Trade
2024-04-28.abstract: Reshaping the drug war in one of Central America’s most lawless corners, the fentanyl boom has devastated the trade in opium poppies used to make heroin.

2024-04-28.title: Many Ukrainian Prisoners of War Show Signs of Trauma and Sexual Violence
2024-04-28.abstract: As they return with physical and psychological wounds stemming from torture by their Russian captors, soldiers are being sent back to active duty — often without adequate treatment.

2024-04-29.title: TikTok Star Is Killed in Third Death of Social Media Influencer in Iraq
2024-04-29.abstract: The shooting of Ghufran Mahdi Sawadi, known online as Um Fahad, comes amid tightening laws and increasingly conservative attitudes in the country.

2024-04-29.title: What to Know: First Trial in Alleged Coup Plot in Germany Begins
2024-04-29.abstract: Three trials this spring are centered on the Reichsbürger movement. Some of its members say the modern German state is actually a corporation run by shadowy bureaucrats.

2024-04-29.title: As Anger Grows Over Gaza, Arab Leaders Crack Down on Protests
2024-04-29.abstract: Grief and rage over the war and Israel have led to demonstrations across the Arab world. Arrests suggest governments fear the outrage could boomerang.

2024-04-29.title: Blinken Meets With Arab Officials to Discuss Gaza and Postwar Plans
2024-04-29.abstract: “The only thing standing between the people of Gaza and a cease-fire is Hamas,” said Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken.

2024-04-29.title: Philippines Closes Schools as Heat Soars to ‘Danger’ Level
2024-04-29.abstract: Scorching temperatures coincided with a nationwide strike of jeepneys, the main mode of public transport in the country.

2024-04-30.title: Chad Election 2024: What to Know
2024-04-30.abstract: The Central African nation’s May 6 election appears to offer voters a choice. But it’s been masterminded, analysts say, to rubber-stamp the rule of the incumbent, Mahamat Idriss Déby.

2024-04-30.title: A Proposed Law Targeting ‘Foreign Interests’ in Georgia Riles the Opposition
2024-04-30.abstract: Opponents of the measure, which resembles a Russian law that Moscow has used to crack down on dissidents, say it could undermine efforts for Georgia to join the European Union.

2024-04-30.title: Blinken Arrives in Jordan to Discuss Aid for Gaza
2024-04-30.abstract: The visit comes as the Biden administration is trying to increase pressure on Hamas to accept a deal that would result in a temporary cease-fire in the war in Gaza and the release of some hostages.

2024-04-30.title: Child Killed in London Sword Attack, Police Say
2024-04-30.abstract: A 14-year-old boy was killed and several others were hospitalized in a stabbing spree in northeast London, the police said. A 36-year-old man was arrested.

2024-04-30.title: Hamas and Fatah Officials, Longtime Rivals, Met in China
2024-04-30.abstract: The Chinese government did not say when the talks between the main Palestinian factions were held. Expectations for progress were low.
